steps.title: Expand the factored form and match coefficients
steps.content:
Expand $(hx + k)(x + j)$:
$$hx^2 + (hj + k)x + kj$$
This must equal $5x^2 + bx - 48$. Comparing coefficients:
  • Coefficient of $x^2$: $h = 5$
  • Coefficient of $x$: $hj + k = b$
  • Constant term: $kj = -48$

steps.title: Use kj = -48 to identify which expression is always an integer
steps.content:
Since $k$ and $j$ are integers with $kj = -48$, $k$ must be a divisor of $48$.

Check each choice:
  • "$\frac{48}{h}$" $\frac{48}{h} = \frac{48}{5}$ is not an integer since $5 \nmid 48$.
  • "$\frac{b}{h}$" $\frac{b}{h} = \frac{5j+k}{5}$ depends on $k$. If $k = 6$ and $j = -8$, then $b = 5(-8) + 6 = -34$, so $\frac{-34}{5}$ is not an integer.
  • "$\frac{b}{k}$" $\frac{b}{k} = \frac{5j+k}{k} = 5\frac{j}{k} + 1$ is not always an integer. If $k = 6$ and $j = -8$, then $5\frac{-8}{6} + 1 = \frac{-40+6}{6} = \frac{-34}{6}$, not an integer.
  • "$\frac{48}{k}$" $\frac{48}{k}$ is always an integer because $k$ divides $48$. For any divisor of $48$ (e.g., $\pm 1, \pm 2, \pm 3, \pm 4, \pm 6, \pm 8, \pm 12, \pm 16, \pm 24, \pm 48$), the quotient $\frac{48}{k}$ is an integer.
That matches the correct answer: D is correct.
